title: claude-dotfiles — Shared Configuration
description: Portable settings that live in your private config repo. Defines canonical variable names and their meanings — actual paths stay in machine.json on each machine. Safe to commit; contains no paths or secrets.

variables.description: Canonical variable names and what they represent. Keys are variable names — they become $NAME in Claude sessions and must match keys in every machine's machine.json. Values are human-readable descriptions only, never paths. This file defines the vocabulary; machine.json defines the local translation.

additionalProperties.description: What this variable represents — not a path.

commands_enabled.description: Which built-in commands from the public claude-dotfiles repo to activate. Command names match filenames in commands/ without the .md extension. Your private repo can add its own commands alongside these.

preferences.description: Behavioural preferences applied across all Claude sessions on any machine using this shared config.

greeting.description: Show a daily session greeting summarising date, priorities, and open items.

greeting_once_per_day.description: Show the greeting only on the first session of each calendar day. If false, greeting runs every session.

seclog_on_session_start.description: Run /seclog automatically at the start of every session that does not begin with the full greeting.
